title: Ross-on-Wye, Symonds Yat & Tintern Abbey

main_route.excerpt: We left at 5.30 + reached The White House at Ross at 6.45. It's on the side of the hill overlooking the Wye.
on to Tintern Abbey. Gorgeous ride thro spring green colours. V. cold. Peaceful Abbey, well worth a visit.
Simon & Eve & Tinder had lunch in the van at Westbury Court Garden.

excursions.label: Yat Rock and Symonds Yat (Friday evening)

stops.excerpt: We walked from this view-point Yat Rock down to river + up again.
Then we went in John's whizzy turquoise car to Symonds Yat. Fantastic views. Strenuous walk.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page B_0248_R shares a boundary with a preceding entry
mixed_end: page B_0250_L shares a boundary with a following entry
summary: A long weekend in April 2015 with Simon, Eve and the dog Tinder staying at The White House B&B in Ross-on-Wye, with day trips to Symonds Yat (including a visit with son John on the Saturday). On the Sunday they visited Tintern Abbey and Westbury Court Garden before returning home to Birmingham.
extraction_notes: The date on B_0248_R was transcribed with iso 2009-04-24, but the trip_id and the Sunday date (B_0249_L, iso 2015-04-26) confirm the year is 2015; April 24th 2015 was indeed a Friday. John is identified as one of the sons visiting from the Mumbles (South Wales), not a through-traveller. The Saturday excursion to Symonds Yat is recorded as a second visit — the Friday evening walk to Yat Rock and the Saturday trip with John are treated as separate excursions from the Ross base. Westbury Court Garden (National Trust, near Westbury-on-Severn, Gloucestershire) was a lunch stop on the homeward route from Tintern. No crossings: the entire trip is within England and Wales.
